principle:
Default TRANSFORM = 5 for any performer credited as puppeteer/operator of a puppet character (Muppets, Sesame Street, Fraggle Rock, Greg the Bunny) whenever they operate the puppet off screen or from within/behind it, with their face and body never visible on screen in that role. A puppet is total substitution of screen presence, like a full creature suit, matching the scale's own definition of 5. It cannot be 4: 4 requires "at most the eyes or mouth remain visibly their own," but a puppeteer's eyes/mouth are never on screen. The archive's uniform filing at 4 undercounts this; all 17 cards here fit the clean case and correct to 5.

Exceptions:
1. On-camera crossover — if the performer also appears un-puppeteered on screen in the credited role, score that appearance independently; don't blend with the puppet portion.
2. Voice-only, no puppet operation — if the credit is pure voice work for a puppet/suit operated by someone else, there is no on-screen design attributable to this performer; flag as out-of-scope, not 5. UC-260 mixes both: Dr. Blinky puppet work scores 5; the Ewok-voice half (suit worn by another performer) is voice-only, not independently scored.
reasoning: Dan Milano performs Greg the Bunny as puppet; his own face/body never on screen in this role. Clean case of family principle default.
